title: Digitaria tonsa
taxonomy: Digitaria tonsa is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses, and belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae. This species is a member of the genus Digitaria, which includes over 300 species of grasses. The genus Digitaria is further divided into several subgenera, with Digitaria tonsa being part of the subgenus Pachycladon. Digitaria tonsa is a type of grass that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Its classification also places it within the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plant species.

intro: Digitaria tonsa, also known as African couch grass or tonson's finger grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Digitaria tonsa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Digitaria tonsa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
